conflict_type: Conflicts between Keyword Constraints and Phrase Constraints
seed_instruction: Write a email to your professor to ask for an extention on a project due in two days.
org_constraint: The phrase "I sincerely apologize" must appear exactly once.
new_constraint: Avoid using the keyword "apologize" in the email.
instruction: Write a email to your professor to ask for an extention on a project due in two days. The phrase "I sincerely apologize" must appear exactly once. Avoid using the keyword "apologize" in the email.
response:
Subject: Request for Extension - [Project Name] - [Your Name]

Dear Professor [Professor's Last Name],

I hope this email finds you well.

I am writing to respectfully request a short extension on the [Project Name] project, which is currently due in two days, on [Original Due Date].

I have been making significant progress on the project; however, due to [briefly and professionally state the reason, e.g., an unforeseen personal commitment, a heavier than anticipated workload this week, a technical issue with my research data], I haven't been able to dedicate the necessary time to ensure the quality of my final submission meets your standards.

I sincerely apologize for any inconvenience this request may cause.

Would it be possible to have an extension until [Propose a specific new date, e.g., end of the week, Monday, October 30th]? I am confident that with a few extra days, I can submit a thorough and high-quality piece of work.

Thank you very much for considering my request and for your understanding.

Best regards,

[Your Name]
[Your Student ID Number]<turn|>
